chunks.doc_name: Top MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies
chunks.text: # Top 30 MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies ![Top 30 MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies-1](https://www.greenlight.guru/hs-fs/hubfs/Top%2030%20MDCG%20Guidance%20Documents%20for%20Medical%20Device%20Companies-1.png?width=800&height=400&name=Top%2030%20MDCG%20Guidance%20Documents%20for%20Medical%20Device%20Companies-1.png) ![Top 30 MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies-1](https://www.greenlight.guru/hs-fs/hubfs/Top%2030%20MDCG%20Guidance%20Documents%20for%20Medical%20Device%20Companies-1.png?width=800&height=400&name=Top%2030%20MDCG%20Guidance%20Documents%20for%20Medical%20Device%20Companies-1.png) The [Medical Device Coordination Group](https://health.ec.europa.eu/medical-devices-dialogue-between-interested-parties/medical-device-coordination-group-working-groups_en#:~:text=The%20Medical%20Device%20Coordination%20Group,new%20technologies%20and%20clinical%20investigation.) (MDCG) is a regulatory body made up of industry experts appointed by each of the member states of the European Union. Broadly speaking, the goal of the MDCG is to help ensure that both EU MDR and EU IVDR are implemented efficiently and effectively. To that end, the MDCG has put out over 100 guidance documents for medical device manufacturers, notified bodies, and other stakeholders. The MDCG guidance documents elaborate on and clarify various aspects of [EU MDR](/blog/device-class-requirements-eu-mdr) and [EU IVDR](/blog/ivdr-in-vitro-diagnostic-medical-device), and they’re an invaluable resource for anyone involved in placing devices on the market in the EU. While we encourage you to check out the [full list of MDCG guidance documents](https://health.ec.europa.eu/medical-devices-sector/new-regulations/guidance-mdcg-endorsed-documents-and-other-guidance_en), this blog provides you with a list of our **top 30 MDCG guidance documents**—the documents that are most often searched for and/or widely applicable across device types and risk classes. Use this list to find the MDCG’s answers to some of the most-asked questions about EU MDR and IVDR. Happy scrolling! ## Breakdown of the top 30 MDCG guidance documents [**FREE EBOOK**: Click here to download a PDF version of the Top 30 MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies.](#popup) | system [MDCG 2018-1 rev. 4](#MDCG-2018-1-rev-4) Guidance on basic UDI-DI and changes to UDI-DI [MDCG 2018-5](#MDCG-2018-5) UDI assignment to medical device software [MDCG 2019-7](#MDCG-2019-7) Guidance on article 15 of the medical device regulation (MDR) and in vitro diagnostic device regulation (IVDR) on a ‘person responsible for regulatory compliance’ (PRRC) ### MDCG 2022-5 **Who is this guidance for?** Anyone who is unsure of whether their product is a medical device or a medicinal product and needs to understand which regulations will govern their product. **Category:** Borderline and Classification **Publication date:** April 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-5](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-04/mdcg_2022-5_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-24 **Who is this guidance for?** Any manufacturer who is planning on marketing their medical device in the EU and needs to know what risk class their device falls under. **Category:** Borderline and Classification **Publication date:** October 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-24](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-10/mdcg_2021-24_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-28 **Who is this guidance for?** Any sponsor of a clinical investigation that needs to notify a Member State of a substantial modification of their clinical investigation. Once the EUDAMED module on Clinical Investigation & Performance Studies is ready, notification of substantial modification to a clinical investigation will occur through EUDAMED. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** December 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-28](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-01/mdcg_2021-28_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-6 **Who is this guidance for?** Sponsors of clinical investigations of medical devices that are being carried out under EU MDR. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2021 ### MDCG 2020-8 **Who is this guidance for?** Any medical device manufacturer that is required to perform a PMCF and compile the data in a PMCF evaluation report. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2020 ### MDCG 2020-7 **Who is this guidance for?** Any medical device manufacturer that is required to perform a PMCF can use this document to help them compile their PMCF plan in a manner that complies with EU MDR. **Category:** Clinical

chunks.doc_name: Top MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies
chunks.text: is required to perform a PMCF and compile the data in a PMCF evaluation report. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2020 ### MDCG 2020-7 **Who is this guidance for?** Any medical device manufacturer that is required to perform a PMCF can use this document to help them compile their PMCF plan in a manner that complies with EU MDR. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-7](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2020_7_guidance_pmcf_plan_template_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-6 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of legacy devices that were CE marked under the MDD or AIMDD and now must undergo the conformity assessment procedure according to EU MDR. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-6](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2020_6_guidance_sufficient_clinical_evidence_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-5 **Who is this guidance for?** Medical device manufacturers who want to use data from an existing device on the market for the purpose of CE marking, and need to demonstrate equivalence with that device. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** April 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-5](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2020_5_guidance_clinical_evaluation_equivalence_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-13 **Who is this guidance for?** Notified bodies that need to clearly document the outcome of their assessment of a manufacturer’s CER. The template may also be useful to manufacturers who want to better understand how their CER will be assessed. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** July 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-13](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-07/md_2020-13-cea-report-template_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-9 rev. 1 **Who is this guidance for?** Both manufacturers who need to create SSCPs and the notified bodies that will review the documents. **Category:** Clinical investigation and evaluation **Publication date:** March 2022 ### MDCG 2021-3 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of custom-made devices or those who want to know if their device falls under the category of custom-made. **Category:** Custom-made devices **Publication date:** March 2021 ### MDCG 2021-1 rev. 1 **Who is this guidance for?** Member states, notified bodies, and manufacturers who need to meet their obligations under EU MDR while various EUDAMED modules are still being developed. **Category:** EUDAMED **Publication date:** May 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-1 rev. 1](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-05/2021-1_guidance-administrative-practices_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-5 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of legacy devices that will continue to be placed on the market after the EU MDR date of application. **Category:** EUDAMED **Publication date:** April 2019 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-5](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2019_5_legacy_devices_registration_eudamed_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2022-16 **Who is this guidance for?** Authorized representatives, manufacturers, and any other economic operators who need to understand the role of the authorized representative and the regulatory requirements they operate under. **Category:** Authorized Representatives, Importers, Distributors **Publication date:** October 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-16](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-10/mdcg_202216_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-26 **Who is this guidance for?** Any economic operators engaged in repackaging or relabelling activities who need to understand and clarify their obligations under the Regulations. **Category:** Authorized Representatives, Importers, Distributors **Publication date:** October 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-26](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-10/md_mdcg_2021_26_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-16 rev. 1 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers and notified bodies that need to understand how to classify an IVD before it is placed on the market, as well as regulators and other stakeholders who

chunks.doc_name: Top MDCG Guidance Documents for Medical Device Companies
chunks.text: activities who need to understand and clarify their obligations under the Regulations. **Category:** Authorized Representatives, Importers, Distributors **Publication date:** October 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-26](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-10/md_mdcg_2021_26_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-16 rev. 1 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers and notified bodies that need to understand how to classify an IVD before it is placed on the market, as well as regulators and other stakeholders who need to assess the class attributed to an IVD. **Category:** In vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVD) **Publication date:** January 2022 ### MDCG 2022-2 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers, investigators, and study sponsors of IVDs in the EU. **Category:** In vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVD) **Publication date:** January 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-2](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-01/mdcg_2022-2_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2022-8 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers with legacy devices that need to know which IVDR requirements are applicable to their devices. **Category:** In vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVD) **Publication date:** May 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-8](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-05/mdcg_2022-8_en.pdf) ### SaMD Infographic **Who is this guidance for?** Any software manufacturer who is unsure of how their product will be classified in the EU. **Category:** New technologies **Publication date:** March 2021 **Link to guidance:** [Is your software a medical device?](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-03/md_mdcg_2021_mdsw_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-1 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of MDSW that need to understand what type of clinical evidence their product will require to comply with EU MDR or EU IVDR. **Category:** New technologies **Publication date:** March 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-1](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2020_1_guidance_clinic_eva_md_software_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-16 rev. 1 **Who is this guidance for?** Medical device manufacturers in the EU whose devices include any risks related to cybersecurity, including those that incorporate electronic programmable systems and those that are MDSW. **Category:** New technologies **Publication date:** December 2019 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-16 rev. 1](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2022-01/md_cybersecurity_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-11 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers or developers of software that are unsure of whether their product will be categorized as Medical Device Software (MDSW) and subject to regulation under EU MDR or EU IVDR. **Category:** New technologies **Publication date:** October 2019 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-11](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2019_11_guidance_qualification_classification_software_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-6 rev. 4 **Who is this guidance for?** Notified bodies and anyone who wants to better understand their role under the Regulations. **Category:** Notified bodies **Publication date:** October 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-6 rev. 4](https://health.ec.europa.eu/document/download/9c9c532f-013a-477c-9378-0a9e714e5549_en?filename=md_mdcg_qa_requirements_notified_bodies_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2020-3 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers and notified bodies who need to understand whether a change to a given device will constitute a significant change under EU MDR. **Category:** Notified bodies **Publication date:** March 2020 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2020-3](https://health.ec.europa.eu/document/download/48d19004-1f88-4da0-9c93-84c9495ba1cf_en?filename=md_mdcg_guidance_significant_changes_annexes_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-13 **Who is this guidance for?** Notified bodies that need clarification on the sampling requirements for their assessment of the technical documentation of a manufacturer’s medical device. **Category:** Notified bodies **Publication date:** December 2019 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-13](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2019_13_sampling_mdr_ivdr_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2022-21 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of devices that require a PSUR, which includes all risk classes except Class I devices. **Category:** Post-Market Surveillance and Vigilance (PMSV) **Publication date:** December 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-21](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-01/mdcg_2022-21_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-19 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers

2019-13](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2019_13_sampling_mdr_ivdr_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2022-21 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of devices that require a PSUR, which includes all risk classes except Class I devices. **Category:** Post-Market Surveillance and Vigilance (PMSV) **Publication date:** December 2022 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2022-21](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-01/mdcg_2022-21_en.pdf) ### MDCG 2021-19 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers with questions about the UDI requirements in the Regulations and how they should implement them. **Category:** Unique Device Identifier (UDI) **Publication date:** July 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2021-19](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-07/md_2021-19_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2018-1 rev. 4 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers who are new to the concept of Basic UDI-DI and want to get a basic understanding of what it is and how it works. **Category:** Unique Device Identifier (UDI) **Publication date:** April 2021 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2018-1 rev. 4](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2021-04/md_mdcg_2018-1_guidance_udi-di_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2018-5 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers of MDSW who need to understand how UDI rules apply to their software and what to consider in regards to UDI when making changes to software. **Category:** Unique Device Identifier (UDI) **Publication date:** October 2018 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2018-5](https://health.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-09/md_mdcg_2018_5_software_en_0.pdf) ### MDCG 2019-7 **Who is this guidance for?** Manufacturers who are unclear as to their obligations when it comes to the [PRRC](/blog/prrc-eu-mdr-ivdr) or a PRRC themselves who want to gain more clarity about their role. **Category:** Other topics **Publication date:** June 2019 **Link to guidance:** [MDCG 2019-7](https://health.ec.europa.eu/document/download/463b4f08-44a2-4018-9957-488bf386fc3a_en?filename=md_mdcg_2019_7_guidance_art15_mdr_ivdr_en.pdf) [**FREE EBOOK**: Click here to
